app.title: Oubaitori Collective Blossom Trail
app.subtitle: It is Okay to be Here.
app.welcomeMsg: Join us on the Oubaitori Collective Blossom Trail as we gather anonymous evidence for the design of a new support service for neurodivergent adults across the North East of England and beyond. Not able to join us? We would still love to hear your views. Complete the questions in the survey below. Your views matter to us!

s0.title: OUR VISION
s0.narrative: Supporting neurodivergent adults to recover, regulate and thrive. Bloom at your own pace, supported by a collective where no one walks alone.

s1.title: YOUR JOURNEY BEGINS
s1.techText: Identify 5 items you see, 4 things you can touch, 3 sounds you hear, 2 scents you can smell, and 1 flavour you can taste. Anchors you in the present.

s2.title: A MOMENT TO CHECK IN
s2.techText: Inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 4 seconds, exhale for 4 seconds, hold for 4 seconds. Regulates heart rate.

s3.title: UNDERSTANDING WHAT YOU NEED
s3.narrative: Noticing what would bring you the most comfort and ease right now.
s3.techText: Run cold water over wrists for 30s to help reset a triggered nervous system.

s4.title: UNDERSTANDING YOUR CAPACITY
s4.narrative: Respecting your natural rhythm and where your energy sits today.
s4.techText: Give yourself a firm hug. Deep pressure helps the brain process where the body is in space.

s5.title: RECOGNISING YOUR STRENGTHS
s5.narrative: A shift toward resilience, agency, and thriving.
s5.techText: Hum a low note to feel the vibration in your chest. Stimulates the vagus nerve to soothe stress.

s6.title: EXPLORING WHAT COULD SUPPORT YOU
s6.narrative: Opening up to the people, spaces, and ideas that can walk alongside you.
s6.techText: By tensing and relaxing different muscle groups, you can discharge stored tension and increase body awareness.

s7.title: FINDING WHAT IS RIGHT FOR YOU
s7.narrative: In your own time, at your own pace, no comparison.
s7.techText: Deliberately shaking your arms, legs, or whole body for 1 to 2 minutes can help release excess adrenaline and restore a sense of calm.

survey.title: Blossom Trail Survey
survey.heading:
Oubaitori Collective
Blossom Trail Survey
survey.success: Your response has been recorded successfully.
survey.errorRequired: Please complete this question to continue.

q1.title: 1. How do you describe your gender?
q1.desc: Understanding the gender of potential service users helps us to design and deliver gender-appropriate services
q1.placeholder: Please specify if 'Prefer to self-describe' selected

q2.title: 2. Tell us your age
q2.desc: Understanding the age of potential service users helps us to design and deliver age-appropriate services

q3.title: 3. How are you feeling?
q3.desc: Most people have a mix of feelings from day-to-day. Tell us how you have been feeling overall in the last 4 weeks
q3.blooming: Blooming – I am thriving and living authentically
q3.stretched: Stretched – My energy is dipping and day-to-day tasks feel harder
q3.strained: Strained – I am feeling pressure from demands and using all my energy to manage daily tasks
q3.overloaded: Overloaded – I am struggling daily and just about functioning
q3.burnout: Burnout – I am completely overwhelmed and exhausted. I am unable to function

q4.title: 4. Where do you live or work?
q4.desc: We would like to understand more about where potential service users live and how services may vary in your local council area

q5.title: 5. Your current circumstances
q5.desc: Select the option that best describes your circumstances right now
q5.employment: I am in employment (paid work, including apprenticeships)
q5.selfEmployed: I am self-employed (sole trader, running a business)
q5.education: I am in full-time education or training
q5.notEmployed: I am not in employment, education or training right now

q6.title: 6. My daily environments, where I live, work and socialise
q6.desc: The places I spend time in feel right for me and meet my unique needs

q7.title: 7. My energy levels
q7.desc: I feel confident that my daily activities are sustainable without risking my energy levels or health

q8.title: 8. Daily Activities
q8.desc: What specific daily tasks drain your energy the most?

q10.title: 10. Support Services
q10.desc: I have been able to access support services in my area that understand my neurodivergence and/or burnout

q11.title: 11. What works for you?
q11.desc: What tools and techniques do you use to help you function?

q12.title: 12. What would help you or others?
q12.desc: Understanding what would help you and other neurodivergent adults will help us to design services that meet people's needs
q12.livedExperience: Peer led mentoring lived experience training
q12.paidWorkTrials: Paid work trials to gain experience
q12.unsure: I am not sure; however, I know I need some support

q13.title: 13. Community Based Services
q13.desc: Are there any services, groups or tools (digital or internet based) in your area that ARE working well for you?
